summary: The notebook demonstrates how to initialize and interact with the Kaggle chess environment using `kaggle_environments`, including overriding the default starting position with a custom FEN string for the Ruy López opening. It introduces a conceptual Gaussian framework for modeling player skill ratings, illustrating how wins and losses update the mean skill and uncertainty over time. Finally, it provides a rule-based submission agent that prioritizes checkmates, captures, and promotions before defaulting to random moves. Readers should learn how to set up the competition environment, visualize skill uncertainty dynamics, and implement a basic heuristic bot.
competition_flow: Initializes the Kaggle chess environment, sets a custom opening via FEN notation, visualizes a Gaussian skill-rating update mechanism, and implements a rule-based submission agent that prioritizes checkmates and captures before submitting moves.
data_reading: Uses `kaggle_environments.make()` to instantiate the environment and parses board states from FEN strings via the `Chessnut` library within the submission agent.
data_processing: Overrides the default board state using a custom FEN string; no additional preprocessing, normalization, or augmentation is applied.
insights: FEN notation can be used to override the default starting position in the Kaggle chess environment.
Player skill and uncertainty can be effectively modeled as a Gaussian distribution where wins increase the mean rating and decrease uncertainty.
A simple heuristic prioritizing checkmates, captures, and promotions provides a functional baseline for early-game decision making.
